transcript: Um. Okay! If you wanna maybe call back and speak to a manager, um, I don’t know if you wanna get your money back or something, but that sounds pretty serious, so I would let him know also. I’ll also let him know. His name is Paul and he’s here in the mornings. I mean, I’ll let him know right now that you—
[“Man(1)”, an employee of Buddy’s Brews on Carson, enters call]
…that this happened, but he won’t be here until tomorrow morning. But I’m gonna let him know. You wanna—
Sorry, what are you talking about?
This— Some guy just told me he had one of our cigars and it—it exploded in his face. This is a business.
Okay, so we a— Someone called our number saying that one of our cigars exploded in his face. Who was I just talking to?
I have no idea, ma’am! Uh, you— I just picked up the phone here. I haven’t called anybody. I don’t call anybody from this number—
Really? Our phone rand and someone was complaining about our cigars and then it exploded in his face and then you started talking. Okay! That was really weird. [inhales]
[“Man(2)”, an employee of an unknown establishment, enters call]
[unclear] Beer, how can I help you?
Yeah, no, I—the best thing to do, I guess, would be to call—I—I—I can’t do anything for you because I’m just a worker bee, here—
[“Woman(2)”, an employee of McNeilly Beer, enters call]

But, um, John and Amanda, who own it, will be here tomorrow. You could try either stopping in or calling and explaining it to them and then—I don’t really have that authority to tell you what I—what can be done or can’t be done—
Hi! Are you calling MeNeilly Beer?
No! I—I had a c—in—this is an incoming call that came in to my shop.
Oh! No, we just answered [laughs] your phone call!
Oh, well, I’m not sure what happened, because I had a gentleman on the line who was calling in to me.
Oh! Sorry! Yeah, no, this is—this is a beer store.
Perry Beer jumped on the phone. I don’t know where that came from. They’re a beer distributor off, ah, Route 19 in Ross Township.
So if you call back tomorrow after 11 or stop back in the shop and then, you know, explain to them what happened and show ‘em your receipt, then, you know, tha—that decision is—is up to them.
[“Man(3)”m an employee of Liberty Beer, enters call]
Liberty Beer. How can I help you?
No, I’m sorry. This— For some reason, this call keeps jumpin’ all over the place and I—I was— I have a call—thi—I—this call came in to my shop! [chuckles]
Oh, weird! Yeah, I don’t know about that!
Alright! Alright, well, have a good day!
Well that was another beer distributor that picked up the phone, again. I don’t know what’s going on.
[“Woman(3)”, an employee of Beer on Butler, enters call]
Yeah, I’m sorry. It— This phone keeps switchin’ to people. I’m not sure what’s going on, but [inhales]—
—I’m—I’m— This call— I don’t know—it’s almost like a party line. [chuckles] So—
I don’t think we’re trying to get ahold of you.
[approximates “continual sfx”] You should’ve brought it back in! I’ve never heard of a cigar doing that.
[“Woman(3)”, an employee of Beer on Butler, enters call]
Hi! Beer on Butler! [speaking to customer] You’re good to go, honey. [to “Woman(4)”] Hello?
I—I didn’t call ya! I don’t know what number—what—who’s— I don’t don’t know what— You called me. Yeah.
Your phone keeps calling us! This is like the third time this number has called us!
Well, that’s very strange! [laughs] This is a—a regular landline. I was just—
…speaking to a gentleman. That’s weird! I was literally just speaking to a guy and you came in!
Yeah, your phone keeps callin’ us for some reason.
Oh, that is very strange! Sorry ‘bout that—
Okay, well have a good night!—
I don’t know what the— You too, honey!
I was talking to you and Beer on Butler kicked in!

I mean, honestly, honey, I mean, I don’t know what to tell ya. I’ve never heard of that in my entire life. I’ve been in this business since 2009. I’ve never heard of a cigar blowing up on ya. Um, you should have actually brought it in to show me. Do you remember what kinda cigar it was? A—cigars don’t blow up.
Honey, I could never give you a refund if we don’t even know what kind it is and I didn’t see the cigar.
[“Duano”, an employee of Smokin’ Brews(?), enters call]
I’m sorry. Something’s going on. Some guy keeps calling me and then other people come in! This is weird!
I don’t— I don’t know! [stammers] You know, you gotta be careful now, you know. ‘Cause there’s scammers always callin’—
This is a business place, here.
Oh, so somebody call you and our phone rang.
‘Cause yo— Now— He called me a second ago and it was Beer on Butler that kicked in.
I know! That’s what I said! This— Whoever this guy is calling me, his phone is doing something. So it’s calling me—
…and then I’m talking to him and then—now you just kicked in and started talking—
…and then—but prior to this, Beer on Butler! She said, “Beer on Butler”. And I’m like,—
“Well, where’d the guy go?” It’s very strange!
We just got a phone call here. Some guy saying—
…that some—a cigar did something to him—
Cigar blew up in his face and, yeah—
…he wanted a refund and all that—
And he’s like, “I have my receipt”. I’m like, “Dude, I don’t give out receipts”. I said, and, “I’ve been in the business since 2009—“
“I’ve never heard of a cigar blowing up on somebody”. Um—
Yeah, “You shoulda”— He said said he threw it down the drain—
I said, “well then you shoulda brought it back. I can’t give you a refund on something that I never seen and I don’t know what kind it was”. [laughs] I mean, so—
Hey, Duano! What’s goin’ on, honey?
They’re tryin’ to scam cigar places.
Why are answering Smokin’ Brewers phone?
Because I wanted to talk to that hacker because here’s the thing: I’m— On the weekends, my job is to destroy them.

I didn’t even catch your voice! That’s funny! But Duano—which is weird, though, but how is it that he’s calling me—okay?—then you come in and say “Smokin’ Brewers”. That’s werid! Literally a second ago, he—
They’re just trying to get— They’re scamming!
Yeah, I get it! But then, how is the phone ringing at somebo— at— I don’t understand what going— Because literally I’m talkin’ to him like I’m talking to you right now—
[voicemail of Bloom Cigar Company, Inc. enters call]
Thank you for calling Bloom Cigar Company. Our hours—
Wait, don’t say nothin’. I’m gonna deal with it—
I’m not! Can you hear it, though?—
…you are socially distanced and wear a mask when you come in the store. Have a good day! [beep]
[voicemail of Bloom Cigar Company, Inc. terminates]
And you hear—? Do you hear—
…like, a robot sound in the background?
Can you hear what’s going on right now, though? It’s a robot sound.
I’m gonna— Listen, let me hang up, ‘cause I need to find this number. I ha— I’m gonna find them.
I’m gonna call back too. Okay, honey!
Don’t call—don’t call them back! No, don’t do that!—
That’s the l—last thing you wanna do—
